title: rubus horridus var. pygmaeus
intro: Rubus horridus var. pygmaeus, also known as Dwarf Giant Blackberry, is a small shrub native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine, valued for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The medicinal properties of Rubus horridus var. pygmaeus have been found to promote wound healing, reduce swelling and pain, and exhibit antioxidant activity, providing potential health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and enhanced immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions for topical or internal use, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
